w:r.w:t: South Africa's Gateway to Digital Transformation Education

Ginashe Digital Academy is a South African digital skills institution dedicated to making Digital Transformation a reality — from school leavers to enterprise executives. All programmes are structured against the South African Qualifications Authority (SAQA) NQF framework, mapped to SETA unit standards, and designed for submission to the Quality Council for Trades and Occupations (QCTO) for formal accreditation.

The academy offers 7 learning tracks spanning the full digital economy, each running across 4 progressive levels. Learners can enter at any level appropriate to their prior experience and qualifications, and stack credentials as they progress.
The table below shows the full curriculum architecture. Each column represents a level; each row a learning track. ★ indicates the first course to be fully developed.
How AI works · Prompt craft · Ethics
Kali Linux · Pen testing · CompTIA S+

Designed for school leavers, first-job seekers, and individuals entering the digital economy with no prior specialist knowledge. No formal qualifications beyond Grade 9–11 are required. Courses at this level provide practical, job-ready skills in 8–12 weeks, and are ideal for TVET partnerships, YES programme participants, and government-funded learnership pipelines.

Targets early-career professionals, career changers, and upskilling workers in the first 1–3 years of their career. Entry requires a Foundation certificate or equivalent work experience. Courses run 10–16 weeks and prepare learners for vendor associate certifications (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, CompTIA, Microsoft).

For mid-career specialists, senior practitioners, and team leads seeking to deepen expertise or formalise experience. Courses run 12–20 weeks, include capstone projects, and align with professional certifications (AWS Solutions Architect, CISSP, Azure Expert, etc.). Learners should have 2+ years of industry experience or hold an Associate-level certificate.

Executive and senior leadership programmes. Targets CTOs, CISOs, CDOs, senior engineers, and strategic leaders responsible for digital transformation at organisational scale. Delivered in cohort or modular format over 16–24 weeks. Includes executive workshops, industry mentors, and board-presentation practicals.

All GDA qualifications will be registered on the National Qualifications Framework (NQF) via SAQA. Each course maps to formal NQF unit standards with clearly stated exit-level outcomes, assessment criteria, and credit values. This enables learners to accumulate credits toward formal qualifications recognised by employers and government.
Quality Council for Trades and Occupations (QCTO)

Foundation, Associate, and Professional courses will be submitted to QCTO for occupational qualification registration. This places GDA graduates on recognised occupational pathways relevant to ICT, digital media, and business services — sectors identified by the South African Government's Operation Vulindlela and Digital Economy Strategy.

Courses are mapped to unit standards under MICT SETA (Media, Information and Communication Technology) and SERVICES SETA where applicable. This enables employer Skills Development Levies (SDL) to fund learner bursaries, and qualifies GDA as a provider for W&RSETA, BANKSETA, and other sector SETAs.

Curriculum content and assessments are designed to be relevant across Sub-Saharan African markets. The academy will pursue mutual recognition agreements with equivalent bodies in SADC, ECOWAS, and EAC member states, supporting learner mobility and cross-border employment opportunities in the growing African digital economy.
SADC Qualifications Framework (SADCQF) alignment pursued from Year 2
ECOWAS digital skills framework compatibility built into all Foundation and Associate curricula
Partnership pathway with African Union Commission's Digital Transformation Strategy
Content localised for South Africa with adaptation guides for Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, and Zimbabwe
Each course below is mapped to a SETA NQF level and designed to stack progressively within its track. Courses are suitable for accreditation under QCTO and SAQA frameworks.
★ First course to be fully developed

The gateway to cloud computing, designed for school leavers and early-career individuals. Covers fundamentals of cloud infrastructure, networking, security, and Linux. Fully mapped to SETA unit standards. Prepares learners for AWS Cloud Practitioner or Microsoft Azure Fundamentals certifications.
Explain core cloud concepts: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Navigate the Linux command line confidently
Understand IP networking, DNS, and firewalls
Describe shared responsibility in cloud security
Deploy a simple workload on AWS or Azure

Builds on foundation skills with hands-on experience in cloud service configuration, identity and access management, object storage, and compute services. Aligned to AWS Cloud Practitioner and Azure Fundamentals certification tracks.
Configure IAM users, roles, and policies
Work with object and block storage services
Monitor cloud resources and set billing alerts
Understand cloud compliance and shared responsibility

Equips learners to design resilient, scalable cloud architectures. Covers high-availability patterns, disaster recovery, multi-region deployments, and cost optimisation. Aligned to AWS Solutions Architect Associate and Azure Solutions Architect Expert.
Optimise cloud spend and resource utilisation
Senior-level programme for architects and technology leaders. Covers enterprise cloud governance, FinOps practices, landing zone design, multi-cloud strategy, and organisational cloud maturity.
Define cloud governance policies and guardrails
Design and implement enterprise landing zones
Lead FinOps practices across business units
Present cloud strategy to executive stakeholders

An accessible introduction to artificial intelligence for non-technical learners. Covers how AI works, the role of data, everyday AI applications, prompt engineering, and responsible AI use. No prior coding required.
Describe what AI, ML, and deep learning are
Use generative AI tools productively and safely
Identify AI applications in everyday business
Understand basic AI ethics and bias
Craft effective prompts for AI tools
Practical machine learning using Python. Covers data wrangling, supervised and unsupervised learning, model evaluation, and interpretation. Uses scikit-learn and real South African datasets.
Prepare and clean data for ML pipelines
Train and evaluate classification and regression models
Interpret model outputs and communicate results
Use Python, pandas, and scikit-learn fluently
Engineering-level course covering large language models, retrieval-augmented generation, AI APIs, vector databases, and MLOps. Learners build production-ready AI-powered applications.
Integrate LLM APIs into real applications
Build RAG systems with vector databases
Evaluate LLM outputs for quality and safety
Apply responsible AI principles in production
Executive-level programme for leaders driving AI adoption. Covers AI governance frameworks, measuring ROI on AI investments, responsible AI policies, and managing AI-related organisational change.
Develop an enterprise AI strategy and roadmap
Build AI governance and ethics frameworks
Measure and communicate AI business value

Digital safety fundamentals for all workers. Covers common threats, safe browsing, password security, phishing awareness, device security, and data protection under POPIA. No technical background needed.
Identify common cybersecurity threats and scams
Apply strong password and authentication practices
Understand rights and obligations under POPIA

Hands-on penetration testing and ethical hacking. Uses Kali Linux to perform reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, and reporting. Aligned to CompTIA Security+ certification and EC-Council CEH fundamentals.
Use Kali Linux tools for scanning and exploitation
Develops SOC analyst and security engineer skills. Covers SIEM platforms, log analysis, threat hunting, incident response playbooks, and digital forensics basics. Aligned to real-world SOC workflows.
Operate a SIEM and analyse security events
Develop and execute incident response playbooks
Perform threat hunting and log forensics
Collaborate across technical and business teams during incidents

Prepares experienced security professionals for executive leadership roles. Covers risk management frameworks, POPIA and GDPR compliance, ISO 27001 implementation, board-level reporting, and security programme governance.
Navigate POPIA, GDPR, and sector regulations
Report security posture to boards and executives
Build and mentor high-performance security teams

Foundational data skills for the modern workplace. Covers spreadsheets, charts, basic SQL queries, understanding dashboards, and data-driven decision-making. Tailored for South African business contexts.
Read and create data charts and dashboards
Use spreadsheets for data analysis and visualisation

Hands-on business intelligence and analytics. Covers Power BI dashboards, Python for data analysis, descriptive statistics, and storytelling with data. Uses real African economic and business datasets.
Analyse datasets with Python and pandas
Apply descriptive statistics to business problems
Create compelling data narratives for non-technical audiences

Modern data engineering practice. Covers building ETL/ELT pipelines, dbt for data modelling, Apache Spark, cloud data warehouses (BigQuery/Snowflake/Redshift), and pipeline orchestration with Airflow.
Design and build production data pipelines
Model data with dbt in a cloud warehouse
Implement data quality checks and monitoring
Executive and senior practitioner course on next-generation data strategy. Covers CDO competencies, data mesh architecture, AI-augmented analytics, data governance, and monetising data assets.
Develop an enterprise data strategy and roadmap
Design data mesh and data product architectures
Lead data governance and quality programmes
Integrate AI into analytics workflows at scale
Quantify and communicate the value of data assets

Essential digital workplace skills for all employees. Covers Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, professional digital communication, cybersafe habits, online collaboration tools, and productivity fundamentals.
Use M365 and Google Workspace confidently
Communicate professionally via email and messaging platforms
Collaborate effectively using online tools and video conferencing
Manage files and documents in the cloud
Maintain safe digital habits in the workplace

Practical course in automating and digitising business processes. Covers no-code and low-code platforms (Power Automate, Zapier, Bubble), business process mapping, and selecting digitisation opportunities.
Map and analyse current business processes
Build automation workflows with no-code tools
Implement simple business applications with low-code platforms
Measure the impact of process digitisation

Develops the skills to lead digital transformation programmes. Covers change management, building the DX business case, customer experience redesign, stakeholder engagement, and building digital culture.
Build and present a digital transformation business case
Lead organisational change management for DX
Apply CX design thinking in digital projects
Develop and communicate a DX roadmap

Comprehensive executive programme for Chief Digital Officers and senior transformation leaders. Covers enterprise digital strategy, innovation management, digital KPIs, ecosystem partnerships, and measuring digital maturity.
Define and execute enterprise digital strategy
Build and lead cross-functional transformation offices
Manage innovation portfolios and emerging tech adoption
Design digital KPI frameworks and maturity models
Represent digital strategy at board level

First steps into software development. Covers HTML/CSS for web pages, Python programming basics, Git version control, and an introduction to databases. Designed for absolute beginners with no prior coding experience.
Build and style basic web pages with HTML and CSS
Write Python scripts to automate simple tasks
Use Git for version control and collaboration
Understand and query a simple relational database
Debug and troubleshoot basic code problems

End-to-end web development covering React front-end, Node.js back-end, RESTful APIs, and relational and NoSQL databases. Learners build and deploy a full-stack web application as the capstone project.
Build responsive UIs with React and modern CSS
Develop RESTful APIs with Node.js and Express
Design and query relational and document databases
Deploy a full-stack application to the cloud
Modern DevOps and cloud-native engineering. Covers Docker containerisation, Kubernetes orchestration, CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions), infrastructure as code with Terraform, and observability.
Deploy and manage workloads on Kubernetes
Implement monitoring, logging, and alerting systems
Senior engineering programme on building internal developer platforms, SRE practices, platform product management, and FinOps. Prepares engineering leads to drive platform strategy at scale.
Design and build internal developer platforms
Apply SRE principles: SLOs, SLAs, error budgets
Lead FinOps practices for engineering teams
Drive platform adoption and developer experience
Manage platform roadmaps and stakeholder expectations
Start and grow a digital venture from scratch. Covers digital business models, building an online presence, basic SEO, social media for business, and tools for managing a micro-enterprise online.
Define a viable digital business model
Build a basic website or online store
Apply SEO fundamentals to improve online visibility
Use social media to grow an audience and sell
Manage digital tools for a micro-enterprise
Grow and scale an online business. Covers Shopify store management, paid advertising (Google and Meta Ads), email marketing, social media strategy, and reading analytics to improve performance.
Launch and manage a Shopify e-commerce store
Plan and run Google and Meta ad campaigns
Build and automate email marketing workflows
Analyse web and social analytics for insights

Develop skills to lead digital product and growth strategy. Covers product management methodologies, growth hacking, revenue model design, digital partnerships, and competitive analysis in African digital markets.
Define and manage a digital product roadmap
Apply agile and lean product development methods
Identify growth levers and execute growth experiments
Analyse competitive dynamics in African digital markets

Executive programme for founders, investors, and corporate innovation leaders. Covers venture building, startup fundraising, scaling across African markets, corporate innovation programmes, and impact measurement.
Build and pitch a venture-scale digital business
Navigate fundraising from angels to Series A
Develop a market entry strategy for African expansion
Measure social and economic impact of digital ventures
Making Digital Transformation a Reality — for Every South African
